lines.english: How many people visited the Great Exhibition of 1851?
lines.chinese: 有多少人参观了1851年的万国博览会？
lines.raw: How many people visited the Great Exhibition of 1851? | 有多少人参观了1851年的万国博览会？

lines.english: Perhaps the most extraordinary building of the 19th century was the Crystal Palace
lines.chinese: 19世纪最不同寻常的建筑，或许就是水晶宫了，
lines.raw: Perhaps the most extraordinary building of the 19th century was the Crystal Palace | 19世纪最不同寻常的建筑，或许就是水晶宫了，

lines.english: which was built in Hyde Park for the Great Exhibition of 1851.
lines.chinese: 它是为1851年的万国博览会在海德公园建造的。
lines.raw: which was built in Hyde Park for the Great Exhibition of 1851. | 它是为1851年的万国博览会在海德公园建造的。

lines.english: The Crystal Palace was different from all other buildings in the world, for it was made of iron and glass.
lines.chinese: 水晶宫与世界上所有其他建筑都不同，因为它是由钢铁和玻璃建造的。
lines.raw: The Crystal Palace was different from all other buildings in the world, for it was made of iron and glass. | 水晶宫与世界上所有其他建筑都不同，因为它是由钢铁和玻璃建造的。

lines.english: It was one of the biggest buildings of all time and a lot of people from many countries came to see it.
lines.chinese: 它是迄今为止最大的建筑之一，许多国家的人们都前来参观。
lines.raw: It was one of the biggest buildings of all time and a lot of people from many countries came to see it. | 它是迄今为止最大的建筑之一，许多国家的人们都前来参观。

lines.english: A great many goods were sent to the exhibition from various parts of the world.
lines.chinese: 许多商品从世界各地运到博览会展出。
lines.raw: A great many goods were sent to the exhibition from various parts of the world. | 许多商品从世界各地运到博览会展出。

lines.english: There was also a great deal of machinery on display.
lines.raw: There was also a great deal of machinery on display. | 还有大量机器展出。

lines.english: The most wonderful piece of machinery on show was Nasmyth's steam hammer.
lines.chinese: 展出的最精彩的机器是纳史密斯蒸汽锤。
lines.raw: The most wonderful piece of machinery on show was Nasmyth's steam hammer. | 展出的最精彩的机器是纳史密斯蒸汽锤。

lines.english: Though in those days travelling was not as easy as it is today,
lines.chinese: 尽管在那些日子里旅行不像今天这样容易，
lines.raw: Though in those days travelling was not as easy as it is today, | 尽管在那些日子里旅行不像今天这样容易，

lines.english: steam boats carried thousands of visitors across the Channel from Europe.
lines.chinese: 汽船还是载着成千上万的游客从欧洲渡过英吉利海峡。
lines.raw: steam boats carried thousands of visitors across the Channel from Europe. | 汽船还是载着成千上万的游客从欧洲渡过英吉利海峡。

lines.english: On arriving in England, they were, taken to the Crystal Palace by train.
lines.chinese: 抵达英国后，他们乘火车前往水晶宫。
lines.raw: On arriving in England, they were, taken to the Crystal Palace by train. | 抵达英国后，他们乘火车前往水晶宫。

lines.english: There were six million visitors in all,
lines.raw: There were six million visitors in all, | 总共有六百万游客，

lines.english: and the profits from the exhibition were used to build museums and colleges.
lines.chinese: 博览会的利润被用于建造博物馆和学院。
lines.raw: and the profits from the exhibition were used to build museums and colleges. | 博览会的利润被用于建造博物馆和学院。

lines.english: Later, the Crystal Palace was moved to South London.
lines.chinese: 后来，水晶宫被搬到了伦敦南部。
lines.raw: Later, the Crystal Palace was moved to South London. | 后来，水晶宫被搬到了伦敦南部。

lines.english: It remained one of the most famous buildings in the world until it was burnt down in 1936.
lines.chinese: 它一直是世界上最著名的建筑之一，直到1936年被烧毁。
lines.raw: It remained one of the most famous buildings in the world until it was burnt down in 1936. | 它一直是世界上最著名的建筑之一，直到1936年被烧毁。
